title: neonauclea nitida
intro: Neonauclea nitida, also known as the hairy mahang tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Neonauclea nitida include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are typically used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
